meta.title: SpaceX Launch Calendar
meta.description: Subscribe to an online ICS calendar and keep up with upcoming SpaceX launches.

header.copy: Subscribe to an online ICS calendar and keep up with upcoming SpaceX launches.

subscribe.title: Subscribe to Online Calendar
subscribe.copy: Use the links below to subscribe to this calendar on your device. Your calendar client will keep automatically syncing with SpaceX's official launch schedule in real-time.

hero.loadingDescription: Fetching the current mission queue and launch windows from SpaceX website feeds.

hero.unavailableDescription: The app could not reach the SpaceX mission feeds just now.

calendar.noLaunches: No dated launches are available for this month yet.

mission.selectionTitle: Select a mission on the calendar
mission.selectionCopy: Choose a launch from the calendar list to view SpaceX mission details, webcast links, and launch timelines here.
mission.detailsUnavailable: Mission details are unavailable right now.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: FAQ & Guides
faq.q1: How do I add the SpaceX launch schedule to Google Calendar or Apple Calendar?
faq.a1: Extremely easy! Simply click the "Subscribe to Calendar" button at the top of the page. On iOS or macOS devices, it automatically prompts you to subscribe. On Google Calendar, copy the {link} link, navigate to Calendar Web → Other Calendars → Add by URL, and paste the address.
faq.q2: How often is this launch calendar updated?
faq.a2: The schedules are automatically kept updated. The server-side edge engine queries official SpaceX APIs every 5 minutes and caches the latest queues inside Cloudflare KV, updating countdowns and calendar feeds in real-time.
faq.q3: What launch vehicles and launch sites are tracked?
faq.a3: The calendar tracks all active SpaceX launch systems, including Falcon 9, Falcon Heavy, and the deep-space Starship vehicle. Pad locations cover all commercial pads at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station (Florida), Vandenberg Space Force Base (California), and Boca Chica Starbase (Texas).
faq.q4: Can I watch launch live streams directly from the calendar?
faq.a4: Yes. Once SpaceX publishes the official webcast media links, our SWR cache automatically matches details and appends the streaming webcast URLs (such as YouTube or X.com live broadcasts) inside the calendar description and page details panel.
